title: EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - 211info
text: NAACP EUGENE-SPRINGFIELD EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring, mentoring, college preparation, and academic and social support to students. Offers a Youth Council program to connect and motivate students toward collective community involvement, cultural pride, and strong cross-cultural relationships. 330 High Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Students K-12, with emphasis on communities of color and households who qualify for free and reduced lunches. Parents are required to participate in Parent Council once per month. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (541) 484-1119 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 484-1119 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for more information FEES: Donations accepted. Becoming a member of the NAACP is encouraged. Annual membership fees are $30 for adults and $10 for those under age 18 DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Lane County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Eugene Downtown Bus Station is one block away from the office Services: Parent/Family Involvement in Education School Readiness Programs Social Skills Training Tutoring Services Youth Enrichment Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - MIMS HOUSE FOOD PANTRY If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NAACP EUGENE-SPRINGFIELD
